Energy markets spiking hard across $SPY, $USO, and $BNO.

posts.body:
AI infrastructure demand continues to tell a bigger story. 
 
TD Cowen highlighted that $META, $MSFT, and $GOOGL drove a record 9.6 GW of Q2 data center leasing, with another 12.5 GW currently in the pipeline. 
 
That’s a massive amount of capacity being built before considering the next wave of AI adoption. OpenAI also raising its 2030 infrastructure roadmap to 30 GW shows just how aggressive the race for compute is becoming. 
 
The AI trade is no longer just about chips. It’s about power, data

posts.body: $SPY $QQQ $IWM Sitting on my hands and reducing churn lately as I’m heavily allocated to growth equities, but the macro setup warrants caution right now. Not to sound overly bearish, but the geopolitical risk premium is flashing red with the Bab-el-Mandeb Strait closure threatening a supply chain and energy shock, especially while the US SPR sits at 50-year lows, leaving an extremely limited buffer for oil price spikes. The tape can obviously pivot overnight, but the risk/reward favors patience

$TSLA 
 
Earnings are coming Wednesday. 
 
Wall Street is looking for a strong Q2 report, with expectations calling for record revenue around $27.6B, representing roughly 23% year-over-year growth. 
 
The focus won’t just be the headline numbers. 
 
Investors will be watching margins, deliveries, energy business growth, AI progress, and management’s outlook for the second half of the year. 
 
Tesla earnings usually bring major volatility. 
 
Keeping $TSLA on watch for the next big move.

Is This Investing Or Just Betting On The Future? 
 
$ORCL

This isn’t a broken startup. It’s a 48-year-old enterprise software giant. 
 
The market is questioning the balance sheet: 
 
• Free cash flow: -$23.7B 
• CapEx: $56B, up 162% YoY 
• Net debt: $97.6B + another $40B financing plan 
• S&P downgrade to BBB-, one notch abov

posts.body:
Google announced plans to build a new data center in Michigan in partnership with DTE Energy, committing to enable 2.7 GW of new clean energy to support the local grid. 
The project, under evaluation in Van Buren Township, will add 24/7 clean power—including solar, advanced storage, and demand flexibility—through a Clean Capacity Acceleration Agreement, supporting the state’s transition away from coal while improving grid reliability.

posts.body:

posts.title:
$NBIS The "Nvidia discloses 9.3% stake" headline is old money, new paperwork, not a fresh buy. 
 
22.26M shares × ~$89.85 = exactly the $2B warrant deal $NVDA announced in March at ~$90. The
posts.body:
$NBIS The "Nvidia discloses 9.3% stake" headline is old money, new paperwork, not a fresh buy. 
 
22.26M shares × ~$89.85 = exactly the $2B warrant deal $NVDA announced in March at ~$90. The July 13 filing just started the SEC disclosure clock (pre-funded warrants become reportable when exercisable). First NVDA involvement was the $700M round back in Dec 2024 at ~$20.

$NVDA filed a 13G showing a 9.3% stake in $NBIS, representing 22.25M Class A shares. 
 
The news is creating excitement, but the details matter. This is not new buying. The position comes from the existing $2B warrant agreement announced March 11 (about 21.06M shares) plus 1.19M previously held shares.

SPY.blurb: Mixed sentiment with slightly more bearish tags; some expect a 5-7% downside to the 200-day moving average.

MU.name: Micron Technology
MU.blurb: Very bullish; strong fundamentals in memory chips, but some caution about sector pressure from Korean pension rebalancing.

SPCX.name: SpaceX
SPCX.blurb: More bearish than bullish; some see it as a 'free fall' while others believe in long-term disruptive tech.

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.blurb: Bullish; users note that current valuation matches COVID and bear market lows, suggesting a buying opportunity.

QQQ.name: Invesco QQQ ETF
QQQ.blurb: Bearish; traders are shorting or expecting further downside, with one user calling for a short.

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.blurb: Bullish; many believe the stock has bottomed and will gap up, but some remain cautious.

ORCL.name: Oracle
ORCL.blurb: Bullish; investors are surprised it's still low and highlight AI compute demand and long-term shareholder value.

SNDK.name: Sandisk
SNDK.blurb: Bullish; price targets of $1700-$1800 after earnings, with some linking it to broader chip sector moves.

DTE.name: DTE Energy
DTE.blurb: 100% bullish in the sample; seen as a growth and income play, possibly benefiting from AI power demand.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.blurb: Bullish; users dismiss recent selloffs as fake and expect a rally, with some mentioning NVIDIA's stake in NBIS.

NBIS.name: Nebius Group
NBIS.blurb: Very bullish; news of NVIDIA's 10% ownership is a huge catalyst, though some are frustrated by price action.

GOOG.name: Alphabet (Google)
GOOG.blurb: Bullish; ahead of earnings, with some linking it to AI investments and big tech capex.

IREN.name: Iris Energy
IREN.blurb: Extremely bullish; price targets of $60-$100, but some call it a 'con artist' stock, indicating high risk.

SOFI.blurb: Bullish; seen as in accumulation phase before a big markup, but some expect a post-earnings dip.

ACHR.blurb: Bullish; strong day with momentum, but not much fundamental discussion.

sentiment.reasoning: The overall mood is cautiously bullish, driven by strong positive sentiment on tech and AI-related stocks like MU, NBIS, and IREN, which have high bullish-to-bearish ratios. However, broad market ETFs like SPY and QQQ show more bearish sentiment, indicating some caution about the broader market direction.

themes.title: AI and semiconductor focus
themes.explanation: Discussions are heavily centered on AI and memory chips, with MU, NVDA, and NBIS seeing high mentions and bullish sentiment. Investors are excited about AI infrastructure spending and new partnerships, like NVIDIA's stake in NBIS.

themes.title: Space and defense stocks
themes.explanation: SpaceX (SPCX) and Rocket Lab (RKLB) are trending, with mixed sentiment. While some are bullish on space exploration, others are skeptical about valuations. The sector is gaining attention due to recent milestones and government contracts.

themes.title: Energy and utilities
themes.explanation: DTE Energy shows a unique 100% bullish sentiment in the sample, suggesting strong confidence in the utility sector. This may be driven by demand for power from AI data centers and a defensive play amid market uncertainty.

themes.title: Meme stock resurgence
themes.explanation: AMC and TSLA are seeing renewed interest, with AMC jumping in mentions and trending on Yahoo Finance. Retail traders are speculating on short squeezes, but the sentiment is mixed as fundamentals remain challenged.

themes.title: Market correction fears
themes.explanation: SPY and QQQ have bearish sentiment, with some users predicting a 5-7% downside to the 200-day moving average. This reflects concerns about overvaluation and potential economic slowdown, contrasting with bullish bets on individual names.

tickers_to_watch.reason: Micron has extremely bullish sentiment (22 bullish vs 2 bearish) and high mention growth. The community sees strong fundamentals in memory chips, though some note recent sector pressure. Earnings and AI demand could drive the price higher.

Nebius Group has a 25:4 bullish ratio and is trending due to news that NVIDIA owns nearly 10% of the company. Investors are excited about this AI infrastructure play, but the stock has been volatile. The institutional backing is a key catalyst.

Iris Energy shows 27 bullish vs 3 bearish tags, the most bullish ratio in the data. The stock is trending on Yahoo Finance and Stocktwits, with price targets of $60-$100. However, some call it a 'con artist' stock, so volatility is high.

Oracle has 15 bullish vs 5 bearish tags and mentions surged from 3 to 72. The community sees value at current levels, citing AI compute demand and a long-term shareholder perspective. Earnings and AI deals could be catalysts.

Tesla has 13 bullish vs 9 bearish and mentions jumped from 8 to 83. Users are predicting a bottom and gap up, but the sentiment is mixed. The stock is also trending on Yahoo Finance, indicating high retail interest.

risk_factors.title: Broad market weakness
risk_factors.explanation: SPY and QQQ have bearish sentiment, with some traders expecting a 5-7% drop. If the broader market corrects, even bullish individual stocks could be dragged down. Investors should be cautious about overexposure to high-beta names.

risk_factors.title: Meme stock volatility
risk_factors.explanation: AMC and TSLA are seeing speculative trading based on short squeezes rather than fundamentals. These stocks can swing wildly, and retail investors may get caught in sharp reversals. The risk is especially high for AMC, which has weak fundamentals.

risk_factors.title: AI hype vs. reality
risk_factors.explanation: While AI stocks like NBIS and IREN have strong bullish sentiment, some skeptics warn of overvaluation. The sector is prone to sharp corrections if earnings disappoint or if AI spending slows. Investors should diversify and not chase hype.

contrarian_views.title: Bearish on SPY/QQQ
contrarian_views.explanation: Despite the overall bullish tilt on individual stocks, some traders are bearish on broad market ETFs, predicting a 5-7% decline. They argue that institutions are using retail liquidity to exit positions, and that the market is due for a correction.

contrarian_views.title: Skepticism on space stocks
contrarian_views.explanation: SpaceX (SPCX) has more bearish than bullish tags (9 vs 17), with some calling it a 'free fall.' Critics question the valuation and timeline for profitability. This contrasts with the bullish sentiment on other space names like RKLB.

contrarian_views.title: Caution on AI infrastructure
contrarian_views.explanation: While many are bullish on NBIS and IREN, some users label them 'con artists' and warn of manipulation. They argue that the recent positive news is being used to distribute shares to retail. This skepticism suggests potential downside risk.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is the surge in AI and semiconductor stocks, led by Micron (MU) with 243 mentions, up from 66 yesterday. The community is overwhelmingly bullish on MU (22 bullish vs 2 bearish), driven by strong fundamentals in memory chips. However, some note that the sector has been pressured by a Korean pension rebalance plan, creating a buying opportunity. NVIDIA (NVDA) also remains a focal point, with users dismissing recent selloffs as 'fake bullshit' and expecting a rally.

Other notable discussions include the sudden spike in Oracle (ORCL) mentions from 3 to 72, with a 15:5 bullish ratio. Investors are surprised it's still low and highlight AI compute demand. Nebius Group (NBIS) is also hot after news that NVIDIA owns nearly 10% of the company, sparking a 25:4 bullish sentiment. Meanwhile, space stocks like SpaceX (SPCX) and Rocket Lab (RKLB) are trending, but SPCX has more bearish tags (17 vs 9), reflecting skepticism about its valuation.

The crowd is divided on broad market direction: while many are bullish on individual AI names, SPY and QQQ have bearish sentiment, with some predicting a 5-7% drop to the 200-day moving average. This tension suggests that investors are hedging their bets—loading up on high-conviction AI plays while fearing a broader correction. The contrarian view on space stocks and AI infrastructure also indicates that not everyone is buying the hype, which could lead to volatility.

overlooked_insights.title: SPCX Unlock Date Moved Forward
overlooked_insights.insight: SpaceX moved the first unlock date for insider shares forward by one week, now set for August 6 (two days after earnings on August 4), instead of the previously expected August 6 unlock with following Monday. This accelerates the potential selling pressure, which users believe explains the recent price collapse as insiders can sell sooner.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User posted: 'elon just moved the first Unlock date forward by 1 week!! earnings on 4th, they can sell first tranche 2-days later. before it was gonna be Aug. 6th, with unlock that next Monday.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage focuses on the seven-day losing streak and the earnings report date, but does not mention the change in the lock-up expiration schedule.

overlooked_insights.title: Nvidia Discloses 9.3% Passive Stake in NBIS
overlooked_insights.insight: Nvidia disclosed in an SEC filing that it holds a 9.3% passive stake in Nebius Group (NBIS), representing 22.26 million shares. Users see this as a massive vote of confidence and expect other institutions to follow, potentially driving further price appreciation.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Multiple user messages: 'NVDA DISCLOSED A 9.3% PASSIVE STAKE IN $NBIS', 'Nvidia just disclosed that it currently owns 22,256,412 shares of Nebius', 'new disclosure of 10% holdings in NBIS is huge'.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines on NBIS focus on the $775M debt deal and analyst upgrades, but none mention the Nvidia stake filing, which is a significant strategic signal.

overlooked_insights.title: Korean Pension Rebalance Pressuring MU
overlooked_insights.insight: Users attribute recent pressure on Micron and other Korean memory stocks to an impending rebalancing of the Korean government pension fund, which plans to reduce domestic holdings by tens of billions of dollars. This is seen as a temporary institutional flow rather than a fundamental deterioration.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User wrote: 'The threat of Korean government’s pension rebalance plan to reduce the domestic holdings to by tens of billions triggered' and 'Kospi will be green' referencing the Korean market.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage cites SK Hynix warnings and general chip sector weakness, but does not connect the selling to a specific pension fund rebalancing event.

overlooked_insights.title: Anthropic Subleases Compute from xAI via Oracle
overlooked_insights.insight: Users claim that Anthropic signed a deal with xAI for compute capacity on xAI's Colossus 1 supercomputer, indicating severe AI compute demand outstripping supply. This deal would benefit Oracle as a key infrastructure provider for both parties, but the specific relationship is not widely reported.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User stated: 'If Anthropic signed a deal with xAI for compute capacity on their Colossus 1, even though xAI is a competitor... it means there's a HUGE demand for AI computing and not enough supply. Everything that Oracle is building'.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines about Oracle focus on its own capex spending and falling stock price, not on private compute-sharing arrangements between major AI labs.

overlooked_insights.title: Google Partnering with DTE on Michigan Data Center
overlooked_insights.insight: Google is partnering with DTE Energy to build a new hyperscale data center in Michigan, involving 2.7 GW of clean energy, a $10M community fund, and Google covering infrastructure costs to save ratepayers $1.7B. This is a specific operational detail not captured in general headlines.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User wrote: '$GOOGL is partnering with $DTE to build a new hyperscale data center in Michigan. The project includes 2.7GW of clean energy and a $10M community fund with Google covering infrastructure costs to save ratepayers ~$1.7B.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream DTE coverage only mentions earnings expectations and neutral analyst ratings, ignoring this large-scale partnership that would drive future utility demand.

overlooked_insights.title: IREN Horizon 1 Handoff Celebrated with Dinner
overlooked_insights.insight: Users report that IREN's Horizon 1 data center has been completed, based on a social media post showing a 'Celebration Dinner.' This milestone suggests the facility is ready for tenant handoff (likely to Microsoft), which would trigger revenue recognition and de-risk the development pipeline.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User posted: 'Looks like Horizon 1 has been COMPLETED. Celebration Dinner posted!' and another user elaborated on 'horizon 1 handoff to microsoft'.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream articles focus on revenue guidance and contract announcements but miss this informal operational milestone that signals near-term execution.

overlooked_insights.title: SOFI Multi-Month Accumulation by Institutions
overlooked_insights.insight: Users observe that SOFI is in a multi-month accumulation phase, where institutions are quietly buying without pushing up the price, preparing for a future markup. This contrasts with the stock's recent sideways/downward price action and retail frustration.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User wrote: 'we are in a multi-month accumulation. institutions are quietly buying without pushing the price up. this prepares the stock for a big price jump, called a markup.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines discuss growth stories and takeover targets, but do not analyze the institutional order flow or accumulation patterns visible on the tape.

overlooked_insights.title: SNDK Forward PE Under 6x and Stock Split
overlooked_insights.insight: Users highlight that Sandisk's forward P/E is under 6x, and a stock split is anticipated, both of which are not mentioned in mainstream coverage. The low multiple combined with AI memory demand could fuel a sharp re-rating and possible short squeeze.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Messages: 'short squeeze, earnings, forward p/e under 6x, stock split… we’re about to go parabolic' and 'Evercore just lifted $SNDK price target to a massive $3,100 implying 100%+ upside'.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream articles ask 'Is It Time to Buy the Dip?' but do not explicitly cite the forward P/E below 6 or pending stock split as specific catalysts.

catalysts.event: Micron share buyback authorization expected to begin after end of July; UBS says company could buy up to 40% of outstanding stock over two years
catalysts.when: End of July 2026 / ongoing over two years
catalysts.expected_impact: Large buyback could provide strong price support and potentially propel shares to $1750 by end of 2027 according to UBS.

catalysts.event: SpaceX first earnings report as public company (maiden earnings)
catalysts.expected_impact: Could be make-or-break for stock; users also note unlock of insider shares two days later on August 6.
Record revenue of ~$27.6B expected; deliveries beat estimates; focus on FSD and robotaxi narrative.
Options activity shows heavy call buying; earnings beat expected to drive stock toward $400 on better AI chip and cloud narrative.
First revenue-generating data center milestone; could trigger upward re-rating of IREN as development risk diminishes.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Micron and Sandisk shares are crashing; bearish sentiment from Michael Burry warning and SK Hynix warning.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Crowd is overwhelmingly bullish, citing rising HBM prices, Korean pension rebalance as temporary, and a buyback catalyst. They believe the crash is a buying opportunity.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The community may have a point on HBM pricing – some users provide specific contract renewal data showing HBM3E and HBM4 prices rising. The pension rebalance is a plausible short-term factor. However, the crash may also reflect genuine demand concerns, so the truth likely lies in between.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Oracle stock is falling due to heavy capex spending and negative cash flow; analysts question the AI infrastructure buildout.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Long-term Oracle shareholders see the capex as a necessary investment that will pay off, similar to the cloud transition. They view the dip as a gift and point to Oracle's history of overcoming skepticism.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's historical perspective is valid – Oracle has successfully navigated previous transitions. However, the scale of current capex ($55.7B spike) is unprecedented, so near-term financial strain is real. The market may be overreacting, but a prolonged downturn is possible until cash flow improves.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Mainstream coverage is cautiously optimistic about SoFi's customer growth, profitability, and potential as a takeover target.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: The Stocktwits community is predominantly bearish and frustrated – the stock is seen as a 'dead horse', trending only on down days, with many complaining about poor performance relative to peers.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's frustration tracks the stock's underperformance, but the accumulation theory suggests professional money may be building positions. Media optimism may be premature if growth decelerates. The next earnings report will likely determine which view is correct.

SPY.summary: The SPY is getting a lot of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions spiking from 58 to 285 in 24 hours. The conversation is heavily bearish, driven by political and geopolitical concerns. Many users are linking SPY to political drama, especially around Trump and tariffs. There's talk of a 5-7% downside to the 200-day moving average, and some believe institutions are trapping retail before a big drop. The war spiral and trade tensions with Canada are also cited as negatives. However, a few voices note that SPY rarely crashes and tends to V-recover to new highs, suggesting that buying puts is risky. Overall, the mood is cautious to bearish, with fear of a correction or continued volatility. Investors should be aware that the community is reacting to headlines and sentiment rather than fundamentals, and the sharp increase in mentions indicates heightened anxiety.

MU.summary: Micron (MU) is seeing a surge in Reddit mentions from 66 to 243 in 24 hours, and Stocktwits sentiment is strongly bullish (22 bullish vs 2 bearish). The community is excited about the AI memory cycle, with many predicting a return above $1000 and even targets of $1500. Users highlight strong fundamentals in the memory segment, though they note a weird sequence of events (Korean pension rebalance) caused a selloff. There's a belief that the summer weakness is a fake-out, similar to last year's DeepSeek scare. Some see MU breaking away from indices and expect 8-10% green days. The stock is back above a $1T market cap, and the focus is on earnings momentum and margins. Overall, the mood is very bullish, with confidence in AI-driven demand for HBM and advanced memory. Investors should know that the community is optimistic but aware of short-term volatility; the bullish case rests on AI infrastructure spending.

SPCX.summary: SPCX is a stock that has recently exploded in online chatter, with Reddit mentions jumping from 43 to 186 in 24 hours, ranking it #3 overall. The conversation is highly polarized. On the bullish side, some users see it as a play on SpaceX, with one comment calling it 'space x is a real company' and linking it to other disruptive tech like Tesla and Nvidia. There are also generic 'Buy buy buy!' calls. However, the bearish sentiment is stronger, with 17 bearish tags vs 9 bullish on Stocktwits. Many users are calling it a scam, with comments like 'idiot scammed' and 'Almost like this was a scam. Crazy.' Others compare it to the DJT stock (Trump Media) and predict a drop to $110. The stock appears to be a micro-cap or speculative name with no clear fundamental connection to SpaceX, leading to skepticism. Investors should be cautious: the hype seems driven by confusion with SpaceX (which is private) and pump-and-dump dynamics. The high Reddit rank suggests momentum, but the negative sentiment and scam allegations are red flags. There is no real news or earnings to support the price. This looks like a classic meme stock with high risk.

MSFT.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is seeing a surge in Reddit mentions, from 25 to 185 in 24 hours, ranking #4. The sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish, with 12 bullish tags vs 7 bearish on Stocktwits. Users are excited about technical patterns, with comments like 'bullish close!!!' and 'Bull Flagging on the Daily.' One user notes that buying MSFT at current levels means paying the same valuation as during Covid lows and the 2022/23 bear market, suggesting it's undervalued. There's also optimism about earnings, with a call for $500 before earnings and $400 calls loaded. Some users mock bears, saying they didn't know the word 'capex' before recently. The discussion also includes mentions of MSFT being replaced by Palantir in Dell's stack, but that's a minor negative. Overall, the community sees MSFT as a strong buy, with technicals improving and valuation attractive. Investors should note that MSFT is a mega-cap with solid fundamentals, AI growth via OpenAI, and a strong cloud business. The positive sentiment is backed by technical analysis and valuation comparisons. However, the rapid increase in mentions could be due to a coordinated push or earnings anticipation. The bearish side is weak, with only a few comments about bears being trapped. Overall, MSFT appears to be a high-quality stock with strong community support.

QQQ.summary: QQQ, the Nasdaq-100 ETF, is seeing a surge in Reddit mentions (84 in 24h, up from 29), ranking 5th overall. The discussion is heavily influenced by macro events, especially geopolitical tensions with Iran and tariff threats from former President Trump. Many traders are bearish, expecting market drops due to these uncertainties. There's also chatter about specific stocks like NVDA, TSLA, and INTC, with some planning to short. The sentiment on Stocktwits is more bearish (13 bearish tags vs 5 bullish). Overall, the community is cautious, with many hedging positions or expecting volatility. Investors should note that QQQ is being used as a proxy for tech and growth stocks, and the sentiment is driven by macro fears rather than company-specific news.

TSLA.summary: TSLA is buzzing with 83 Reddit mentions in 24h, up from just 8 previously, ranking 6th. The sentiment is mixed but leans bullish on Stocktwits (13 bullish vs 9 bearish). Many retail traders are optimistic, expecting a rebound to $440+ by Friday and calling a bottom. Positive news includes Tesla's Cybercab with Starlink V5 integration. However, there's bearish chatter about Elon Musk's involvement with SPCX and potential dilution from unlock schedules. Some traders are skeptical, linking TSLA to political controversies. Overall, the community is excited about Tesla's innovation but wary of volatility. Investors should watch for earnings and unlock events that could move the stock.

ORCL.summary: Oracle is getting a lot of buzz on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions skyrocketing from 3 to 72 in 24 hours. The conversation is overwhelmingly bullish, driven by Oracle's massive AI infrastructure deals. People are excited about the huge demand for AI computing, citing that Oracle's data centers are essentially pre-booked. Long-term holders are reassuring others, pointing to Oracle's resilience through past crises. Some see the current dip as a buying opportunity, echoing Buffett's 'be fearful when others are greedy' sentiment. However, there are skeptics who question the near-term financials, warning that a quick rally is unlikely. A few odd comments, like the taxi driver anecdote, add color but don't change the bullish tone. Overall, the community sees Oracle as a strong AI play with a bright long-term future, but caution is advised for those expecting immediate gains.

SNDK.summary: Sandisk (SNDK) is heating up on social media, with mentions jumping from 18 to 68 in 24 hours. The sentiment is strongly bullish, fueled by expectations of a short squeeze, upcoming earnings, and a low forward P/E under 6x. Traders are also excited about a potential stock split. A price target of $3,100 from Evercore (implying 100%+ upside) is being widely shared. Some see parallels to last year's DeepSeek scare, dismissing current weakness as fake. However, there are cautious voices warning about bearish sentiment and the need for patience. The community is also discussing related stocks like MU and NVDA, indicating a broader semiconductor rally. Overall, the mood is exuberant, but investors should be wary of hype and focus on fundamentals.

DTE.summary: DTE Energy is getting a lot of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions nearly doubling in the last 24 hours. The buzz is overwhelmingly positive, with no bearish tags on Stocktwits. People are excited about a partnership with Google to build a massive data center in Michigan, which includes 2.7 GW of clean energy and a $10 million community fund. This deal is expected to save ratepayers about $1.7 billion. There's also talk about a bullish kicker candlestick pattern and a price target upgrade from CFRA to $162. Some users are optimistic about DTE's growth and income potential, and its gas utility and non-utility businesses add diversification. However, the discussion is relatively thin, so investors should note that DTE is a regulated utility with steady earnings but limited upside compared to high-growth stocks. The stock's recent price action and options activity suggest short-term bullish sentiment, but the long-term story hinges on the data center project and clean energy transition.

NVDA.summary: Nvidia is a hot topic on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions more than doubling from 27 to 64 in 24 hours. The sentiment is mostly bullish, with 14 bullish tags vs 4 bearish on Stocktwits. The biggest news is that Nvidia disclosed a 9.3% passive stake in Nebius (NBIS), an AI infrastructure company, and Jensen Huang is reportedly doubling down on his confidence in Nebius. Some users see this as a strong signal and are betting against shorts. There's also discussion about Nvidia's role in automotive and robotics through BlackBerry QNX, and a comparison to last year's DeepSeek scare, with one user calling it fake and predicting a rally. However, there are bearish voices too, with some pointing to potential summer doldrums and unlock schedules. The overall vibe is that Nvidia remains a leader in AI, and any dips are buying opportunities. Investors should note that Nvidia's valuation is high, and competition from Chinese models is a concern, but the company's ecosystem and partnerships keep it dominant.

NBIS.summary: The online community is absolutely buzzing about NBIS after news broke that NVIDIA disclosed a 9.3% passive stake in the company, with some saying it's actually nearly 10%. This revelation came as a surprise to many, and traders are extremely bullish, expecting the stock to soar. The sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, with many calling for price targets of $200 or even $400 after earnings. There's frustration with short sellers, but the general mood is that this is a game-changing development. Investors should know that NVIDIA's involvement adds credibility and could attract other institutional investors. However, the stock has already moved significantly, so there's risk of a pullback. The community is treating this as a major catalyst, but it's important to do your own research and not get caught up in the hype.

GOOG.summary: Google (GOOG) is getting attention as it prepares to report earnings this Wednesday. The community is generally bullish, citing its strong position in AI and potential for continued outperformance among the Mag 7. There's discussion about Google developing a more efficient AI chip, which could be a competitive advantage. Some traders are linking Google to other stocks like Planet Labs (PL) due to their partnership. However, the sentiment is more measured compared to NBIS, with some caution about the broader market. Investors should watch the earnings report for guidance on AI investments and cloud growth. The stock has been consolidating, and a strong earnings beat could propel it higher. But competition from Microsoft and Amazon remains a concern.

IREN.summary: IREN is getting a lot of buzz on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions jumping from 9 to 37 in 24 hours. The community is excited about the company's progress on its Horizon 1 data center, which appears to be completed, and the potential handoff to Microsoft. There's also speculation about an unnamed leading AI developer as a tenant, possibly Meta or OpenAI. Bullish sentiment is strong, with many predicting the stock will rise to $60 or even $100 soon. However, some users express distrust in Wall Street manipulation, noting that the stock was driven down before suddenly turning bullish. Overall, the discussion focuses on IREN's operational milestones and AI infrastructure play, with investors advised to watch for announcements on tenant handoffs and expansion plans.

SOFI.summary: SOFI has zero Reddit mentions in the last 24 hours, but Stocktwits shows 14 bullish and 5 bearish messages. The community is divided: some see the stock in a multi-month accumulation phase, with institutions quietly buying ahead of a big price jump. Others are cynical, expecting earnings to cause a temporary pop then a close lower. There's a long-term bullish view with price targets of $70 in 3 years, and some compare it to Buffett-style investing. However, negative sentiment highlights poor performance, with SOFI often trending only on down days. The stock is seen as undervalued but frustrating, with a pattern of disappointing earnings reactions. Investors should note the accumulation theory but be cautious of short-term volatility and earnings risks.

ACHR.summary: Archer Aviation (ACHR) is generating buzz on Stocktwits, with a strongly bullish sentiment (25 bullish vs 7 bearish tags). The community is excited about the company's progress in electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, with many expecting the stock to fly this year. Messages highlight large bids coming in, with one user noting a bid for over 105k shares at $5.30, which is seen as a bullish signal. There's a sense of urgency among retail investors, with calls to hold and buy more, warning against selling prematurely. Some users compare ACHR to other momentum stocks like AMC and NUAI, suggesting it could be a big runner. However, the lack of Reddit mentions indicates limited discussion on that platform. Overall, the community is optimistic about Archer's near-term catalysts, including potential FAA certification and commercialization, but investors should be aware of the speculative nature and volatility typical of pre-revenue EVTOL companies.
